title: VAEs for Data Imputation
concept: VAEs can fill in missing data by encoding the available parts and decoding a plausible completion, useful for incomplete datasets.

exercises.prompt: You have a photo with a chunk torn off. What's the best way to guess what was in the torn part?

options.text: Use the visible parts and patterns to imagine a matching piece
Leave the torn part blank forever

exercises.explain: Just like you'd use clues from the rest of the photo to guess the missing piece, a VAE uses the data it can see to fill in the parts it can't.

cards.title: The Puzzle with Missing Pieces
cards.body: Imagine a jigsaw puzzle where a few pieces are missing. You can still guess what those pieces look like by studying the picture around them. VAEs do exactly this with messy, incomplete data — they look at what's there and dream up a sensible fill-in for what's missing!

cards.title: What is Data Imputation with VAEs?
cards.body: Data imputation means filling in missing values in a dataset. A VAE learns the hidden patterns behind data by encoding it into a compressed 'idea' (latent space). When some parts are missing, the VAE encodes the visible parts, finds the closest matching idea, then decodes it into a full, plausible version — filling in the gaps.

cards.title: The Sketch Artist
cards.body: Think of a sketch artist who only sees half of a person's face. Because they've studied thousands of faces before, they can sketch a believable other half that fits naturally. The VAE is that artist — it has 'seen' so much data that it can sketch believable missing pieces that match the pattern of what's already there.

cards.title: Weather Station Gaps
cards.body: A weather station's sensor breaks for a day, leaving a gap in temperature readings. A VAE trained on past weather patterns can encode the days before and after, then decode a smooth, realistic temperature guess for the missing day — instead of just leaving a blank or a wild guess.

cards.title: Myth: It Just Copies Nearby Data
cards.body: Nope! A VAE doesn't just copy the closest known value like a simple average would. It learns deep patterns across the whole dataset, so it can fill gaps in complex data like images or sound in ways that make real sense — not just a lazy average.

cards.title: Blank Fill vs. Smart Fill
cards.body: Compare a simple blank-fill method to a VAE's smart fill.

left.text: Fills gaps with zeros or averages — often looks fake or blocky

right.text: Fills gaps using learned patterns — looks natural and believable

exercises.prompt: Why can a VAE fill in missing data well?

options.text: It learned patterns from lots of similar data before
It asks a human for the answer

exercises.explain: VAEs learn general patterns from training data, so they can generate believable guesses for missing parts based on those learned patterns.

exercises.prompt: A VAE imputing missing data just averages the nearby known values.
exercises.explain: False! VAEs use learned patterns from the whole dataset to generate realistic completions, not just simple averages.

exercises.sentence: To fill in missing data, a VAE encodes the visible parts and then ___ a plausible completion.
exercises.explain: The VAE encodes what it can see into the latent space, then decodes that into a full, plausible version of the data.

exercises.prompt: A photo has its bottom-left corner missing. What would a well-trained VAE most likely do?

options.text: Generate a believable corner that matches the rest of the photo's patterns

exercises.explain: A VAE uses what it learned about typical images to generate a corner that fits naturally with the rest of the photo, not just a blank or a copy.

exercises.prompt: A medical dataset has some patients missing their blood pressure readings. A data scientist wants to use a VAE to fill these gaps in a realistic way. What should the VAE do?

options.text: Encode the known patient data, then decode a plausible blood pressure value that fits the patient's overall pattern
Set all missing values to zero
Delete every patient with missing data
Randomly assign values with no connection to the patient's data

exercises.explain: The VAE encodes the patient's known information into the latent space, then decodes a realistic blood pressure value that matches patterns learned from similar patients — this is the power of VAE-based imputation.
